summary: Frontend regression test for P1 refactor: CreatePostModal and CreateItemModal now follow the strict 'dumb component' contract { isOpen, onClose, onSubmit } with NO internal fetch — parent pages (Feed.jsx / Marketplace.jsx) perform POST /api/feed and POST /api/marketplace via onSubmit. Logged in as teste@lindart.app, skipped onboarding via localStorage, exercised both happy-path submits and close-without-submit flows. All 10 acceptance criteria pass.
critical_code_review_comments: Contract is correctly enforced: CreatePostModal.jsx and CreateItemModal.jsx receive only { isOpen, onClose, onSubmit } and call onSubmit(payload). Submit/loading state, file→base64 conversion, palette hex parsing, and reset-on-success are kept inside the modals (presentation concerns), POST is owned by parents — matches the requested separation of concerns.

Feed.jsx / Marketplace.jsx wire onSubmit with fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/feed|/api/marketplace`) using authHeaders() and propagate Pydantic detail errors via formatApiErrorDetail. New post/item is prepended via setPosts([new, ...arr]) and modal is closed via setShowCreate(false). Correct.

Minor mismatch with the review_request wording: the request mentions submitting an image_url field, but neither modal exposes an image_url input — they only support file upload (image_base64 via FileReader). Backend accepts either, so functionally OK; if image_url input was intended for UX, it is currently missing. Not a regression vs iteration_23.
Suggestion (non-blocking): duplicated handleFile + base64 preview pattern across both modals could be extracted into a useImageUpload hook (carried over from iteration_23 review).
Suggestion (non-blocking): TYPES list duplicated between CreateItemModal.jsx and Marketplace.jsx — extract to a shared constants module.
test_credentials: teste@lindart.app / Teste#2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Onboarding bypass via localStorage 'lindart.onboarding.v1.completed'=1 and 'lindart.tour.v1.seen'=1.
seed_data_creation: Created during this run: 1 feed post (title=TEST_post_p1_refactor) and 1 marketplace item (title=TEST_item_p1_refactor, type=curso, price=99.90). Both via real POST through the modal UI flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: P1 refactor verified end-to-end: modals are truly 'dumb' (no internal fetch). Network listener captured exactly 1 POST /api/feed and 1 POST /api/marketplace ONLY when submit was clicked; close via X / Cancel triggered 0 network calls. After submit, modal closed (isOpen=false) and the new item appeared at the top of its list (initial_feed_posts=0→1; initial_market_items=1→2). To reproduce: login via /api/auth/login, write token to localStorage 'lindart.auth.token', set onboarding flags, navigate to /feed or /marketplace, wait ~2.5s for AuthContext re-hydration before clicking feed-open-create / market-open-create. Modals do NOT expose an image_url input — file upload (base64) is the only path; this is consistent with iteration_23 implementation.
